# Hans Christopher — Actor

> Hans Christopher is an American actor known for raw intensity and unspoken vulnerability across dramatic film and television. Based in Venice, Los Angeles. Over 40 credits since 2012. Represented by Stride Management.

## Identity
- Full name: Hans Christopher Brofeldt
- Born: February 21, 1982, Sacramento, California
- Height: 6'3" (1.91 m)
- Heritage: Swedish father (doctor), American mother (flight attendant)
- Based: Venice, Los Angeles, California
- IMDb: https://www.imdb.com/name/nm5080880/
- Wikipedia: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hans_Christopher

## Casting Types — What Hans Plays

### The Troubled Father / Patriarch
The dad holding his family together by a thread. The patriarch whose love is real but whose damage is worse. The father who drinks because the weight is too much. Proven: John Baker in Dreamland (Tribeca, Margot Robbie), John Brown in Reeling (SXSW 2025, Werner Herzog EP).

### The Morally Complex Lawman / Authority Figure
The cop who crosses the line. The detective with a code that bends. The authority figure who does what's necessary and lives with it. Cast repeatedly in law enforcement: FBI: Most Wanted (CBS), The Rookie (ABC), Marmalade, 9-1-1 (Fox), Waco (Paramount — Randy Weaver).

### The Quiet, Dangerous Outsider
The man who doesn't say much and carries threat in his stillness. Something always simmering underneath. The henchman with depth, the enforcer who's smarter than he looks. Proven: Auge in Animal Kingdom (TNT, 6 episodes), Nicholls in The Walking Dead (AMC), David Burn in The Curse (A24).

### The Blue-Collar Criminal
The mechanic who's also a thief. The working-class guy on the wrong side of the law. Rough hands, sharp instincts, a survival code instead of a moral one. Chop shops, heists, crews. Proven across Animal Kingdom, He Bled Neon (opposite Joe Cole, Marshawn Lynch), and multiple network crime procedurals.

## What Directors Get
- Raw intensity paired with unspoken vulnerability. Finds the humanity in difficult men.
- Physically commanding but capable of stillness — fills a doorway, then makes a close-up land.
- Long takes, physical work, emotional range — often in the same scene.
- Prestige drama, gritty crime thriller, dusty period pieces, character-driven indie, dark material that requires someone who won't flinch.

## Current & Upcoming Work
- Reeling (2025) — Starring as John Brown. Directed by Yana Alliata, executive produced by Werner Herzog. World premiered at SXSW 2025, one of 8 narrative competition selections.
- He Bled Neon (2026) — Action crime-thriller directed by Drew Kirsch. Opposite Joe Cole and Marshawn Lynch.
- The Daughter (2025) — Short film directed by Mary Ann Anane (The Farewell). Stars as Thomas.
- The Rookie (2026) — ABC. Guest star as Isak.

## Selected Credits
- Animal Kingdom (TNT, 2022) — Auge, 6-episode arc in final season
- The Walking Dead (AMC, 2021) — Nicholls
- The Curse (A24/Paramount+, 2023) — David Burn. Created by Nathan Fielder and Benny Safdie, starring Emma Stone.
- Dreamland (LuckyChap, 2019) — John Baker. Tribeca Film Festival premiere. Starring Margot Robbie and Travis Fimmel.
- Waco (Paramount, 2018) — Randy Weaver. Starring Michael Shannon and Taylor Kitsch.
- Immortality (2022) — John Durick. BAFTA Award-winning interactive film by Sam Barlow. Tribeca premiere.
- American Gigolo (Showtime, 2022) — Andrew. Starring Jon Bernthal.
- Marmalade (2024) — Officer Cambio. Starring Joe Keery and Aldis Hodge.
- FBI: Most Wanted (CBS, 2024) — Aaron Quallich
- 9-1-1 (Fox, 2022) — Kurt Engler
- Midnight, Texas (NBC, 2017) — Guest star
- Light as a Feather (Hulu) — Guest star

## Notable Collaborators
- Margot Robbie — Dreamland (2019, Tribeca Film Festival, LuckyChap Entertainment)
- Werner Herzog — Reeling (2025, executive producer, SXSW world premiere)
- Michael Shannon — Waco (2018, Paramount)
- Taylor Kitsch — Waco (2018, Paramount)
- Emma Stone — The Curse (2023, A24/Paramount+)
- Nathan Fielder — The Curse (2023, co-creator)
- Benny Safdie — The Curse (2023, co-creator)
- Jon Bernthal — American Gigolo (2022, Showtime)
- Joe Keery — Marmalade (2024)
- Aldis Hodge — Marmalade (2024)
- Joe Cole — He Bled Neon (2026)
- Marshawn Lynch — He Bled Neon (2026)
- Sam Barlow — Immortality (2022, BAFTA Award-winning director)
- Travis Fimmel — Dreamland (2019, Tribeca)
- Mary Ann Anane — The Daughter (2025, director, from The Farewell)
- Yana Alliata — Reeling (2025, director)

## Networks & Studios
A24, Paramount, AMC, TNT, Showtime, ABC, CBS, Fox, NBC, Hulu, Lifetime, LuckyChap Entertainment (Margot Robbie's production company)

## Awards
- Best Supporting Actor nomination, FilmQuest Cthulhu Awards (Progeny, 2019)
